operator_instances.transformation_goal: Increase data complexity by adding read IDs with more than two separators, testing that the function correctly extracts the last two fields.
operator_instances.rationale: The seed task only tests with exactly two separators (good case) and one separator (bad case). Adding multi-separator inputs ensures the function's split logic works correctly for any number of fields.

visible_state_patch.context_summary:
Add a comment in the main function to illustrate multiple separator scenario:
    # Testing with extra separators
    read_id_extra = 'lib:cell:well:CB_UMI'

new_hidden_tests.description: Read ID with several fields, last two are barcode and UMI
new_hidden_tests.call_code:
umi, cb = get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id, sep)
result = (umi, cb)

semantic_test_specs.semantic_intent: Verify that the function extracts the last two fields regardless of how many separators appear before them.
semantic_test_specs.target_constraint: The function must split on the given separator and return the last element as UMI and second last as barcode.
semantic_test_specs.expected_failure_mode: A naive solution that assumes exactly two separators would fail with IndexError or return wrong fields.
semantic_test_specs.test_case_description: Read ID with three underscores: prefix_CB_UMI

output_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a multi-separator read ID.

operator_instances.transformation_goal: Add Unicode characters in the barcode and UMI to test that the function correctly encodes them to bytes.
operator_instances.rationale: The seed input uses only ASCII. Real scRNA-seq data may contain non-ASCII characters in sample names, requiring correct encoding.

visible_state_patch.context_summary:
Add a comment:
    # Unicode barcode and UMI
    read_id_unicode = 'run:flowcell:lane:tile:CB_µUMI_δ'

new_hidden_tests.description: Read ID with Unicode characters in barcode and UMI
new_hidden_tests.call_code:
umi, cb = get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id, sep)
result = (umi, cb)

semantic_test_specs.semantic_intent: Verify that the function handles Unicode characters and encodes them correctly to UTF-8 bytes.
semantic_test_specs.target_constraint: The function must return bytes objects for both UMI and barcode, preserving the Unicode characters.
semantic_test_specs.expected_failure_mode: A solution that uses .encode('ascii') or ignores encoding would fail or produce wrong bytes.
semantic_test_specs.test_case_description: Read ID with µ and δ characters.

output_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a read ID with Unicode characters.

operator_instances.transformation_goal: Add read IDs with empty fields (consecutive separators) to test that the function correctly handles empty strings as fields.
operator_instances.rationale: In real data, fields may be missing, leading to empty strings. The function should treat them as valid fields.

visible_state_patch.context_summary:
Add a comment:
    # Empty field due to double separator
    read_id_empty = 'prefix__CB_UMI'

new_hidden_tests.description: Read ID with an empty field from consecutive separators
new_hidden_tests.call_code:
umi, cb = get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id, sep)
result = (umi, cb)

semantic_test_specs.semantic_intent: Verify that the function correctly parses read IDs where consecutive separators produce empty string fields.
semantic_test_specs.target_constraint: The split function should include empty strings; the last two fields should still be the barcode and UMI.
semantic_test_specs.expected_failure_mode: A solution that strips empty fields or assumes non-empty fields would give IndexError or wrong fields.
semantic_test_specs.test_case_description: Read ID with two underscores before CB and UMI.

output_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a read ID with consecutive separators.

operator_instances.transformation_goal: Add computational edge case where the read ID consists entirely of separator characters, resulting in empty fields.
operator_instances.rationale: Testing that the function gracefully handles extreme inputs where all fields are empty.

visible_state_patch.context_summary:
Add a comment:
    # Read ID consisting of only separators
    read_id_all_sep = '___'

new_hidden_tests.description: Read ID with only separator characters
new_hidden_tests.call_code:
umi, cb = get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id, sep)
result = (umi, cb)

semantic_test_specs.semantic_intent: Verify that the function returns empty bytes when the read ID contains only separators.
semantic_test_specs.target_constraint: The last two fields after split should be empty strings, which are encoded to empty bytes.
semantic_test_specs.expected_failure_mode: A solution that expects at least one non-empty field might raise an IndexError or produce unexpected output.

output_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a read ID consisting solely of separator characters.

operator_instances.transformation_goal: Add computational edge case where the read ID ends with a separator, leading the last field to be empty.
operator_instances.rationale: Trailing separators can occur in data; the function should correctly handle them.

visible_state_patch.context_summary:
Add a comment:
    # Trailing separator
    read_id_trail = 'prefix_CB_'

new_hidden_tests.description: Read ID ending with a separator
new_hidden_tests.call_code:
umi, cb = get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id, sep)
result = (umi, cb)

semantic_test_specs.semantic_intent: Verify that the function uses the last field (empty) as UMI and second-last as barcode.
semantic_test_specs.target_constraint: The split result includes an empty string at the end; the function must return empty bytes for UMI.
semantic_test_specs.expected_failure_mode: A solution that assumes the last field is non-empty might strip it or raise an error.

output_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a read ID with a trailing separator.

operator_instances.transformation_goal: Add computational edge case where the read ID contains exactly two fields (only barcode and UMI, no prefix).
operator_instances.rationale: Testing that the function works when there is no prefix, which is a valid but distinct scenario.

visible_state_patch.context_summary:
Add a comment:
    # Only barcode and UMI, no prefix
    read_id_two = 'CB_UMI'

new_hidden_tests.description: Read ID with only barcode and UMI
new_hidden_tests.call_code:
umi, cb = get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id, sep)
result = (umi, cb)

semantic_test_specs.semantic_intent: Verify that the function handles read IDs with exactly two fields (no prefix).
semantic_test_specs.target_constraint: The first field becomes cell barcode (second last) and second field becomes UMI (last).
semantic_test_specs.expected_failure_mode: A solution that assumes at least three fields might misinterpret indices.

output_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a read ID with exactly two fields.

operator_instances.transformation_goal: Adversarial test where the separator itself is used as part of the prefix, ensuring the function does not incorrectly match it.
operator_instances.rationale: The function should split on exact separator matches; if the separator appears in the prefix, it should still work correctly.

visible_state_patch.context_summary:
Add a comment:
    # Separator appears in prefix as well
    read_id_adv = 'UMI_CB_UMI'
# Note: separator is '_', but 'UMI' appears as prefix.
# The function should treat the last two fields correctly.

new_hidden_tests.description: Separator appears in prefix, but function extracts last two fields correctly
new_hidden_tests.call_code:
umi, cb = get_cell_umi_read_string(read_id, sep)
result = (umi, cb)

semantic_test_specs.semantic_intent: Ensure the function does not confuse the presence of the separator in the prefix; it must only consider the last two fields after splitting.
semantic_test_specs.target_constraint: The split must be exact; the last field is always UMI, second last is barcode, regardless of prefix content.
semantic_test_specs.expected_failure_mode: A solution that tries to find the separator from the end might incorrectly assume the prefix 'UMI' is part of the barcode or UMI.

input_variant.value: read_id where prefix contains the same string as a field

output_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a read ID where the separator appears in the prefix (e.g., 'UMI_CB_UMI').
